title: lactuca sororia var. pilipes
intro: Lactuca sororia var. pilipes is a lesser-known variety of the common milkweed plant, native to North America, characterized by its hairy stems and dark green leaves. Traditionally, Lactuca sororia var. pilipes has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which have been employed to treat various health issues. The health benefits of this plant are attributed to its high content of saponins, flavonoids, and other bioactive compounds that can help alleviate digestive issues, reduce fever, and boost the immune system. In herbal preparations, Lactuca sororia var. pilipes can be used as a tea, tincture, or infusion, often combined with other herbs to create a medicinal concoction that harnesses its healing properties.
